# services.jellyfin

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.jellyfin`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.jellyfin.cacheDir` | `absolute path` | Directory containing the jellyfin server cache, passed with `--cachedir` see [#cache-directory](https://jellyfin.org/docs/general/administration/configuration/#cache-directory) |
| `services.jellyfin.configDir` | `absolute path` | Directory containing the server configuration files, passed with `--configdir` see [configuration-directory](https://jellyfin.org/docs/general/administration/configuration/#configuration-directory) |
| `services.jellyfin.dataDir` | `absolute path` | Base data directory, passed with `--datadir` see [#data-directory](https://jellyfin.org/docs/general/administration/configuration/#data-directory) |
| `services.jellyfin.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Jellyfin Media Server. |
| `services.jellyfin.forceEncodingConfig` | `boolean` | Whether to overwrite Jellyfin's `encoding.xml` configuration file on each service start. When enabled, the encoding configuration specified in {option}`services.jellyfin.transcoding` and {option}`services.jellyfin.hardwareAcceleration` will be applied on every service restart. A backup of the existing `encoding.xml` will be created at `encoding.xml.backup-$timestamp`. ::: {.warning} Enabling this option means that any changes made to transcoding settings through Jellyfin's web dashboard will be lost on the next service restart. The NixOS configuration becomes the single source of truth for encoding settings. ::: When disabled (the default), the encoding configuration is only written if no `encoding.xml` exists yet. This allows settings to be changed through Jellyfin's web dashboard and persist across restarts, but means the NixOS configuration options will be ignored after the initial setup. |
| `services.jellyfin.group` | `string` | Group under which jellyfin runs. |
| `services.jellyfin.hardwareAcceleration.device` | `null or absolute path` | Path to the hardware acceleration device that Jellyfin should use. For obscure configurations, additional devices can be added via {option}`systemd.services.jellyfin.serviceConfig.DeviceAllow`. |
| `services.jellyfin.hardwareAcceleration.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable hardware acceleration for video transcoding. |
| `services.jellyfin.hardwareAcceleration.type` | `one of "none", "amf", "qsv", "nvenc", "v4l2m2m", "vaapi", "rkmpp"` | The method of hardware acceleration. See [Hardware Acceleration](https://jellyfin.org/docs/general/post-install/transcoding/hardware-acceleration) for more details. |
| `services.jellyfin.logDir` | `absolute path` | Directory where the Jellyfin logs will be stored, passed with `--logdir` see [#log-directory](https://jellyfin.org/docs/general/administration/configuration/#log-directory) |
| `services.jellyfin.openFirewall` | `boolean` | Open the default ports in the firewall for the media server. The HTTP/HTTPS ports can be changed in the Web UI, so this option should only be used if they are unchanged, see [Port Bindings](https://jellyfin.org/docs/general/networking/#port-bindings). |
| `services.jellyfin.package` | `package` | The jellyfin package to use. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.deleteSegments` | `boolean` | Delete transcoding segments when finished. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.enableHardwareEncoding` | `boolean` | Enable hardware encoding for video transcoding. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.enableIntelLowPowerEncoding` | `boolean` | Enable low-power encoding mode for Intel Quick Sync Video. Requires i915 HuC firmware to be configured. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.enableSubtitleExtraction` | `boolean` | Embedded subtitles can be extracted from videos and delivered to clients in plain text, in order to help prevent video transcoding. On some systems this can take a long time and cause video playback to stall during the extraction process. Disable this to have embedded subtitles burned in with video transcoding when they are not natively supported by the client device. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.enableToneMapping` | `boolean` | Enable tone mapping when transcoding HDR content. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.encodingPreset` | `one of "auto", "veryslow", "slower", "slow", "medium", "fast", "faster", "veryfast", "superfast", "ultrafast"` | Encoder preset for transcoding. Lower presets sacrifice quality for speed, higher presets optimize quality. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.h264Crf` | `integer between 0 and 51 (both inclusive)` | Constant Rate Factor (CRF) for H.264 encoding. Lower values result in better quality. Range: 0-51. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.h265Crf` | `integer between 0 and 51 (both inclusive)` | Constant Rate Factor (CRF) for H.265 encoding. Lower values result in better quality. Range: 0-51. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.hardwareDecodingCodecs` | `submodule` | Which codecs to enable for hardware decoding. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.hardwareDecodingCodecs.av1` | `boolean` | Enable hardware decoding for av1 codec. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.hardwareDecodingCodecs.h264` | `boolean` | Enable hardware decoding for h264 codec. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.hardwareDecodingCodecs.hevc` | `boolean` | Enable hardware decoding for hevc codec. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.hardwareDecodingCodecs.hevc10bit` | `boolean` | Enable hardware decoding for hevc10bit codec. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.hardwareDecodingCodecs.hevcRExt10bit` | `boolean` | Enable hardware decoding for hevcRExt10bit codec. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.hardwareDecodingCodecs.hevcRExt12bit` | `boolean` | Enable hardware decoding for hevcRExt12bit codec. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.hardwareDecodingCodecs.mpeg2` | `boolean` | Enable hardware decoding for mpeg2 codec. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.hardwareDecodingCodecs.vc1` | `boolean` | Enable hardware decoding for vc1 codec. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.hardwareDecodingCodecs.vp8` | `boolean` | Enable hardware decoding for vp8 codec. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.hardwareDecodingCodecs.vp9` | `boolean` | Enable hardware decoding for vp9 codec. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.hardwareEncodingCodecs` | `submodule` | Which codecs to enable for hardware encoding. h264 is always enabled. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.hardwareEncodingCodecs.av1` | `boolean` | Enable hardware encoding for av1 codec. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.hardwareEncodingCodecs.hevc` | `boolean` | Enable hardware encoding for hevc codec. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.maxConcurrentStreams` | `null or (positive integer, meaning >0)` | Maximum number of concurrent transcoding streams. Set to null for unlimited (limited by hardware capabilities). |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.threadCount` | `null or (positive integer, meaning >0)` | Number of threads to use when transcoding. Set to null to use automatic detection. |
| `services.jellyfin.transcoding.throttleTranscoding` | `boolean` | When a transcode or remux gets far enough ahead from the current playback position, pause the process so it will consume fewer resources. This is most useful when watching without seeking often. Turn this off if you experience playback issues. |
| `services.jellyfin.user` | `string` | User account under which Jellyfin runs. |
